Code analysis: setup_task.sh line 18 constructs ROOM_URL as '${JITSI_BASE_URL:-http://localhost:8080}/TranslatorPlanning'. With default JITSI_BASE_URL of http://localhost:8080 (as per setup_jitsi.sh line 84), this produces exactly the expected URL

privileged_info_summary: The task 'create_meeting_poll@1' uses a local Jitsi Meet instance running in Docker containers at http://localhost:8080. The room is named 'TranslatorPlanning'. The agent is tasked with creating and publishing a poll with the question 'Which language pair needs additional interpreter staffing for the trade conference?' and four language pair options: 'English - Mandarin Chinese', 'English - Spanish', 'English - Arabic', and 'French - German'. All metadata values are internally consistent with the task description and verified against the setup and verification scripts. The task uses VLM-based verification to confirm the poll is created, published, and visible in the Polls panel. Jitsi Meet's polls feature is a real feature accessed via the overflow menu/toolbar. This is entirely synthetic data running against a containerized local Jitsi Meet instance.
